conversations.content: create a .env file

The Coder agent is creating `.env` and `.env.example` in the project root. The only variable this project needs is `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` — plug in your actual key from [console.anthropic.com](https://console.anthropic.com) and you're set.
restart the server
Dev server restart triggered. Check the Project Preview tab once it's up.
restart server
Dev server has been restarted. Check the Project Preview tab to confirm it's running.

Hey this agent is not running as a managed agent. I think this is just an api call. did I not ask for a managed agent

Attached files (1):
1. /Users/raminhedayatpour/Documents/VibeProjects/ramin-hedayatpour-BFP27fWc/managed-agents-poc-8gnj/.vibing-assets/images/browser-screenshot-1776856968931.png

You're right. Looking at the code, every agent in this POC — orchestrator, research agent, weather agent — uses `anthropic.messages.create()` directly with a hand-rolled while loop. That's just a manual agentic loop, not the Anthropic managed agents API.

The managed agents approach uses the Anthropic Agent SDK's proper agent primitives, where the SDK manages the execution loop for you rather than you implementing it manually. The current code mimics the pattern but isn't actually using the managed agents infrastructure.

Do you want me to refactor this to use the actual Claude managed agents SDK? If so, let me know which agent you want to start with (or all of them), and I'll send it to the Coder.

this whole thing looks messed up. here is what mine looks like that on my other system. You can keep the color you have but the rendering is pretty crap with odd borders and connectors

Attached files (2):
1. /Users/raminhedayatpour/Documents/VibeProjects/ramin-hedayatpour-BFP27fWc/managed-agents-poc-8gnj/.vibing-assets/attachments/attachment-1776867237561-i4jgch951.png
2. /Users/raminhedayatpour/Documents/VibeProjects/ramin-hedayatpour-BFP27fWc/managed-agents-poc-8gnj/.vibing-assets/attachments/attachment-1776867237616-mr9j25k76.png

The fix has been sent to the Coder agent. Three specific changes:

1. **Ghost white rectangle** — The `.react-flow__node` wrapper div has a default white background that bleeds through behind the MUI Paper. New CSS sets it to transparent.
2. **Black border artifact** — React Flow adds an `outline` to selected/focused node wrappers. The CSS override removes it since MUI Paper already handles the selected state visually.
3. **Straight lines with dots** — Adding `defaultEdgeOptions` with `smoothstep` type and `MarkerType.ArrowClosed` markers will give you clean curved edges with arrowheads instead of raw endpoint dots.

Your color scheme stays intact — just the structural rendering gets cleaned up.
